name: com.coachfit.apikey.application.service.ApiKeyService
comment:
Application service implementing {@link ApiKeyUseCase}.

Key lifecycle (docs/08-auth-model.md §API Key Authentication)

  1. Generate: {@code cf_live_} + 32-char secure random hex
  2. Hash: SHA-256(rawKey) → hex string stored as {@code key_hash}
  3. Prefix: first 8 chars of raw key stored as {@code key_prefix} for display
  4. Raw key returned once in {@link CreatedKey} and NEVER stored or logged

name: com.coachfit.shared.adapter.in.security.SecurityConfig
comment:
Core Spring Security configuration for CoachFit.

Filter chain order (docs/08-auth-model.md §Spring Security Filter Chain)

 Request → CorsFilter                     (Spring Security built-in via corsConfigurationSource) → JwtAuthenticationFilter      (order 1 — extract + validate JWT) → ApiKeyAuthenticationFilter (order 2 — fallback if no JWT) → FeatureGateFilter        (order 3 — tier-change blacklist + @RequiresTier) → RateLimitFilter        (order 4 — Redis daily counter + headers) → Controller

Public endpoints (no auth required)

  • {@code POST /api/v1/auth/**} — register, login, refresh, OAuth callbacks
  • {@code GET /api/v1/auth/**} — OAuth initiation flows
  • {@code POST /api/v1/webhooks/**} — Strava, Garmin, Stripe webhooks
  • {@code GET /api/v1/webhooks/**} — Strava webhook verification
  • {@code GET /join/**} — public athlete invite-link acceptance
  • {@code GET /actuator/health} — liveness / readiness probes

methods.name: corsConfigurationSource
methods.comment: CORS configuration sourced from {@code app.url} (docs/08-auth-model.md §Security Checklist: "CORS — Whitelist frontend domain only").

methods.name: passwordEncoder
methods.comment: BCrypt with strength 12 (docs/08-auth-model.md §Security Checklist: "Password storage — BCrypt (strength 12)").

methods.name: restClient
methods.comment: Shared {@link RestClient} instance used by {@code GoogleOAuthService} to call Google's token and userinfo endpoints.

name: com.coachfit.shared.adapter.in.security.apikey.ApiKeyAuthenticationFilter
comment:
Fallback authentication filter for CoachFit API keys.

Runs after {@link com.coachfit.shared.adapter.in.security.jwt.JwtAuthenticationFilter}. Only activates when:

  1. The {@code Authorization} header contains {@code Bearer cf_live_*}
  2. No authentication is already present in the {@link SecurityContextHolder}

API Key format (docs/08-auth-model.md §API Key Authentication)

 Authorization: Bearer cf_live_<32-char hex> Format stored in DB: SHA-256(rawKey) as hex Full key shown only once on creation — never retrievable again.

Authentication flow

  1. Extract raw key from Bearer header (must start with {@code cf_live_})
  2. Hash with SHA-256 via {@link ApiKeyService#sha256Hex}
  3. Look up active, non-expired key in DB via {@link ApiKeyPersistencePort}
  4. Load owning user → build {@link UserPrincipal} → set in context
  5. Update {@code last_used_at} asynchronously

methods.name: touchLastUsedAsync
methods.comment: Updates {@code last_used_at} in a best-effort manner. Runs inline (filter is not async-aware); failure is swallowed to avoid blocking the request or causing auth failures on a non-critical update.

name: com.coachfit.shared.adapter.in.security.jwt.JwtAuthenticationFilter
comment:
First filter in the CoachFit security chain (after CORS).

Extracts a JWT from the {@code Authorization: Bearer } header, validates it via {@link JwtTokenProvider}, and, on success, places a {@link UserPrincipal}-backed {@link UsernamePasswordAuthenticationToken} in the {@link SecurityContextHolder}.

API-key tokens ({@code cf_live_*}) are intentionally skipped here and handled by {@link com.coachfit.shared.adapter.in.security.apikey.ApiKeyAuthenticationFilter}.

On failure the filter simply clears the context and continues the chain — the {@link org.springframework.security.web.AuthenticationEntryPoint} will produce the 401 response for any subsequently protected endpoint.

Filter chain order (docs/08-auth-model.md)

 CorsFilter → JwtAuthenticationFilter → ApiKeyAuthenticationFilter → FeatureGateFilter → RateLimitFilter → Controller
